hawkish_phrases: a more prolonged period of policy restriction was likely to be warranted to mitigate the remaining risks that inflation could settle above the target
I remain concerned about both upside and downside risks to inflation
I remain concerned that inflationary pressures stemming from an overly rapid withdrawal of policy restriction over the past two years still need to be contained
A cut now would overweight the importance of the near-term mechanical disinflation and risk higher term premia
firms in the DMP Survey expect little change in their own-price inflation over the next year
dovish_phrases: Bank Rate is likely to be reduced further
the risk from greater inflation persistence had continued to become less pronounced, while some risks to inflation from weaker demand and a loosening labour market remain
monetary policy was still too restrictive, and a further cut in Bank Rate was warranted at this meeting
I place greater weight on downside risks, particularly in the near term, including those from an elevated household saving rate and a weakening labour market
The market-implied path for Bank Rate looks too tight
summary: The MPC voted 5-4 to hold Bank Rate at 3.75%, with four members preferring a 0.25pp cut to 3.5%, while stating that Bank Rate is likely to be reduced further though judgements around further easing will become a closer call, reflecting reduced inflation persistence risks but ongoing concern about balancing downside risks from weaker demand.
